title: Active Management and Performance Measurement: A fund has an expected return of 0.1 and a...
summary: Calculate portfolio expected return as the weighted average of component asset returns.
prompt: A fund has an expected return of 10% and a standard deviation of 20%. An investor wants to decrease the risk of the portfolio by investing in T-bills with a return of 5%. What proportion should be invested in T-bills to achieve a portfolio return of 8%?

options.text: Calculated using weights and standard deviations
Minimizes risk for a given return
Using the formula for expected return, E(r) = w_f * r_f + w_p * r_p, solve for

solution: Using the formula for expected return, E(r) = w_f * r_f + w_p * r_p, solve for the proportion in T-bills.
